member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Checkpoint.CheckpointData
member.summary: Immutable snapshot of pipeline state written to checkpoint.dat for crash recovery.
member.param: Identifier of the segment that contained the last fully committed record at checkpoint time.
Byte offset within of the last fully committed record.
Sequence number of the last fully committed record.
Identifier of the currently active (head) segment open for writes. May equal when no rollover has occurred since the last checkpoint.

para.b: On-disk format (36 bytes, all fields little-endian):

remarks.code: Offset Size Field 0 4 Magic = 0x504B4843 ("CHKP" LE) 4 1 Version = 1 5 3 Reserved = 0x000000 8 4 LastSegmentId (uint32) 12 8 LastOffset (int64) 20 8 LastSeqNo (uint64) 28 4 ActiveSegmentId (uint32) 32 4 Crc32 (CRC-32/ISO-HDLC over bytes 0–31) Total: 36 bytes
remarks.para: Invariants:

list.item: The CRC covers exactly the first 32 bytes; the last 4 bytes are the CRC itself.
The format is frozen; changes require a new ADR and a version bump.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Checkpoint.CheckpointReader
member.summary: Reads and validates the checkpoint.dat file from a pipeline directory, returning a snapshot or a descriptive failure.

remarks.para: A missing checkpoint.dat is a valid, expected state (first run or no checkpoint written yet). It is surfaced as rather than thrown as an exception, so callers can distinguish it from an I/O error.
The reader ignores checkpoint.tmp: a stale temp file from a prior crash does not affect the result.
Invariants:

list.item: This method never writes to or modifies any file.
It is idempotent: repeated calls return the same result for unchanged files.
A valid checkpoint is never returned silently if the CRC check fails.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Checkpoint.CheckpointReader.Read(System.String)
member.summary: Reads the checkpoint from .
member.param: Absolute path to the pipeline root directory.
member.returns: with the deserialized snapshot on success; with a otherwise.
member.remarks: The CRC check detects bit-rot corruption and complete record substitution. It cannot detect a torn write that overwrites the checkpoint record with bytes whose CRC happens to match — for example, if the page cache flushes a partial record before the atomic File.Move of the temp file completes. In practice, uses write-to-temp + Flush(flushToDisk: true) + File.Move(overwrite: true), which makes torn checkpoint writes extremely unlikely on NTFS and POSIX. This is a residual theoretical risk, not a known practical defect.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Checkpoint.CheckpointWriter
member.summary: Persists a snapshot to disk using a crash-safe write-temp + fsync + rename strategy.

remarks.para: Write sequence:

list.item: Serialize into a 36-byte buffer and write it to checkpoint.tmp in the pipeline directory.
Call FileStream.Flush(flushToDisk: true) to fsync the temp file.
Rename checkpoint.tmp → checkpoint.dat (overwriting any existing checkpoint).

remarks.para: A crash between steps 1 and 3 leaves checkpoint.dat unchanged. A crash during step 1 leaves checkpoint.tmp partial or absent. Neither scenario corrupts the last valid checkpoint.
Failure modes: directory-not-found and I/O errors propagate to the caller unchanged. A stale checkpoint.tmp from a prior crash is silently overwritten.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Checkpoint.CheckpointWriter.Write(System.String,ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Checkpoint.CheckpointData)
member.summary: Atomically writes as the new checkpoint in .
member.param: Absolute path to the pipeline root directory. The directory must exist; it is not created automatically.
The checkpoint state to persist.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.BroadcastRouter
member.summary: Routes every record to every registered sink lane (broadcast / fan-out).
member.remarks: Under , lanes are written sequentially via — a full lane blocks the router until space is available. Under , is used — the record is discarded immediately if the lane is full and is invoked. Under , overflow records are written to the lane's spill file; once a spill file exists all records go to spill (not directly to the channel) to preserve FIFO order. Parallel fan-out is a Phase 3 optimisation.
member.param: Backpressure policy to apply when a lane is at capacity.
Callback invoked with the sink name whenever a record is discarded under . Never called under .

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.ContentTypeRouter
member.summary: Routes records to sink lanes whose registered filter matches the record's . Records that do not match any registered lane filter are dropped and the callback is invoked with sink name "*".

remarks.para: Use (called by Pipeline.AddSink(consumerName, sinkName, sink, ContentType)) to associate each lane with its content-type filter before the pipeline starts.
Lanes registered without an explicit filter (i.e. whose name is not in the filter map) receive every record regardless of content type.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.HashRouter
member.summary: Distributes records across sink lanes by computing ((keySelector(record) % lanes.Count) + lanes.Count) % lanes.Count and writing to the selected lane only. This provides stable, deterministic partitioning for a fixed set of lanes (lanes are frozen at pipeline start).

remarks.para: The double-modulo formula ensures a non-negative index even when returns a negative value.
If the lane list is empty, is a no-op.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.SinkLane
member.summary: A bounded async channel that decouples the record router from a sink task.

remarks.para: Block policy: awaits when the lane is at capacity, suspending the caller until the sink task drains at least one record.

Spill policy: when the lane is full, overflow records are written to a local at the path supplied to the constructor. The reader loop replays spill records back into the channel via during idle time and after each routed record.
is an approximate value under concurrent access. It is suitable for metrics and observability but must not be used for correctness decisions.

member.summary: when a spill file is active for this lane (either because the lane overflowed or because a crash-recovery file was found at startup). Once , all routed records go to spill (never directly to the channel) to preserve FIFO order.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.SinkLane.TryDrainOneSpillRecord
member.summary: Attempts to replay one record from the spill file into the channel.
member.remarks: Must only be called from the reader loop task (the sole channel writer). If the channel is full after a record was read from the file, the read position is rewound so the record is retried on the next call.

member.summary: Signals that no more records will be written to this lane. The sink task will drain remaining records and then observe channel completion.
member.param: Optional fault to surface to the reader; null for a clean completion.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.SpillFile
member.summary: Append-only overflow store for a single . Records written here are replayed back into the lane's channel when space becomes available.

remarks.para: The spill file uses PECL record framing () so that crash-truncated tails are detected via CRC validation during replay.
Compression flags are stripped on write: is always the decoded (decompressed) payload, so the spill file stores uncompressed records.
Two handles are kept open simultaneously: an append-only write handle and a sequential read handle. Only the reader loop task calls and , so no synchronisation is needed.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.SpillFile.CompressionFlags
member.summary: All flag bits related to compression; stripped when appending to spill.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.SpillFile.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Opens (or creates) the spill file at for append and replay. If the file already exists (crash recovery), the read stream starts at offset 0 so all previously-written records are replayed on the first call.
member.param: Absolute path to the spill file.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.SpillFile.Create(System.String)
member.summary: Creates the parent directory (idempotent) then returns a new at . If a file already exists it is opened for crash-recovery replay rather than truncated.
member.param: Absolute path to the spill file.

member.param: The reconstructed on success; otherwise.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.SpillFile.SeekRead(System.Int64)
member.summary: Seeks the read stream to . Used to undo a call when the channel is full, so the record is retried on the next drain attempt.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.SpillFile.DeleteAndDispose
member.summary: Disposes both stream handles then deletes the spill file from disk. Call after full replay is confirmed to reclaim disk space.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorData
member.summary: Immutable snapshot of a consumer's read position, written to {ConsumerName}.cur in the pipeline's cursors/ subdirectory for crash recovery.
member.param: Identifies which consumer owns this cursor. Must be non-empty, valid as a filename component, and encode to no more than UTF-8 bytes.
Identifier of the segment the consumer is currently reading.
Byte offset within of the last successfully processed record.
Sequence number of the last successfully processed record.

para.b: On-disk format (variable length, all fields little-endian):

remarks.code: Offset Size Field 0 4 Magic = 0x53525543 ("CURS" LE) 4 1 Version = 1 5 1 Reserved = 0 6 2 NameLen (uint16, ≤ MaxConsumerNameBytes) 8 NameLen ConsumerName (UTF-8, no null terminator) 8+NameLen 4 SegmentId (uint32) 12+NameLen 8 Offset (int64) 20+NameLen 8 SeqNo (uint64) 28+NameLen 4 Crc32 (CRC-32/ISO-HDLC over bytes 0..(27+NameLen)) Total: 32 + NameLen bytes
remarks.para: Invariants:

list.item: The CRC covers bytes 0 through (27 + NameLen); the last 4 bytes are the CRC itself.
The format is frozen; changes require a new ADR and a version bump.
NameLen is always ≤ .

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorFlusher
member.summary: Manages an in-memory consumer cursor position and flushes it to disk according to a configurable policy: every N records processed, every T time elapsed, or both.

remarks.para: updates the in-memory position and automatically calls to enforce the record-count threshold. For the time-based threshold, the caller must invoke periodically (e.g., from a background timer or the pipeline's main loop).
The flush uses internally, so the write-temp + fsync + rename crash-safety guarantee applies: the on-disk cursor always reflects a committed position, never a partial one.
Invariants:

list.item: is a no-op when no has occurred since the last flush (the cursor is not dirty).
After , all methods throw .
is idempotent.
On graceful shutdown, flushes any dirty state so the on-disk cursor reflects the last advanced position.

remarks.para: Failure modes: I/O exceptions from the underlying propagate unchanged. A flush failure in also propagates.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorFlusher.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.TimeSpan,System.UInt32,System.Int64,System.UInt64,System.Func{System.DateTimeOffset})
member.summary: Initialises a for the named consumer.
member.param: Absolute path to the pipeline's cursors/ subdirectory. Must exist before any flush; it is not created automatically.
Name of the consumer this flusher manages. Must satisfy the same constraints as .
Number of calls that trigger a flush. Must be ≥ 1.
Maximum time between flushes when records are being processed. Must be positive.
Segment ID to seed the in-memory position (default 0).
Byte offset to seed the in-memory position (default 0).
Sequence number to seed the in-memory position (default 0).
Time source for the time-based threshold. Defaults to . Inject a controlled clock in tests to avoid Thread.Sleep.
member.exception: is less than 1, or is not positive.

member.summary: Updates the in-memory cursor position and calls .

Byte offset of the last processed record within the segment.
Sequence number of the last processed record.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorFlusher.FlushIfDue
member.summary: Writes the cursor to disk if the state is dirty and at least one threshold (record count or elapsed time) has been exceeded since the last flush.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorFlusher.Flush
member.summary: Writes the cursor to disk unconditionally if the state is dirty. Does nothing if no has been called since the last flush.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorFlusher.Dispose
member.remarks: Flushes any dirty state before releasing resources. Filtered I/O exceptions (, , ) are silently suppressed — the on-dispose flush is best-effort. Unfiltered exceptions propagate; the flusher is still marked disposed in either case.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorReader
member.summary: Reads and validates the {consumerName}.cur file from the pipeline's cursors directory, returning a snapshot or a descriptive failure.

remarks.para: A missing cursor file is a valid, expected state (new consumer or file deleted). It is surfaced as rather than thrown as an exception, so callers can distinguish it from an I/O error.
The reader ignores any {name}.tmp file: a stale temp file from a prior crash does not affect the result.
Invariants:

list.item: This method never writes to or modifies any file.
It is idempotent: repeated calls return the same result for unchanged files.
A valid cursor is never returned silently if the CRC check fails.
The stored in the file is validated against the requested consumer name; a mismatch is surfaced as an error.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorReader.Read(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Reads the cursor for from .
member.param: Absolute path to the pipeline's cursors/ subdirectory.
Name of the consumer whose cursor file to read. Used to construct the file path ({consumerName}.cur) and to validate the name stored inside the file.
member.returns: with the deserialized snapshot on success; with a otherwise.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorWriter
member.summary: Persists a snapshot to disk using a crash-safe write-temp + fsync + rename strategy.

remarks.para: Write sequence:

list.item: Validate (non-empty, valid filename chars, within UTF-8 bytes).
Serialize into a variable-length buffer and write it to {ConsumerName}.tmp in the cursors directory.
Call FileStream.Flush(flushToDisk: true) to fsync the temp file.
Rename {ConsumerName}.tmp → {ConsumerName}.cur (overwriting any existing cursor).

remarks.para: A crash between steps 2 and 4 leaves {ConsumerName}.cur unchanged. A crash during step 2 leaves {ConsumerName}.tmp partial or absent. Neither scenario corrupts the last valid cursor.
A stale {ConsumerName}.tmp from a prior crash is silently overwritten. The cursors directory must exist; it is not created automatically.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorWriter.Write(System.String,ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Cursors.CursorData)
member.summary: Atomically writes as the new cursor for in .
member.param: Absolute path to the pipeline's cursors/ subdirectory. The directory must exist; it is not created automatically.
The cursor state to persist.
member.exception: is null, empty, contains characters that are invalid in a filename, or encodes to more than UTF-8 bytes.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.PeclErrorCode
member.summary: Identifies the category of a PECL operation failure.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.PeclErrorCode.Unknown
member.summary: Unspecified error — should not occur in practice.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.PeclErrorCode.CrcMismatch
member.summary: The stored CRC32C does not match the independently computed value.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.PeclErrorCode.TruncatedHeader
member.summary: The stream ended before a complete 24-byte record header could be read.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.PeclErrorCode.TruncatedPayload
member.summary: The stream ended before the full payload declared by the header could be read.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.PeclErrorCode.TruncatedFooter
member.summary: The stream ended before a complete 4-byte record footer could be read.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.BackpressurePolicy
member.summary: Controls what happens when a sink lane is at capacity during record routing.
member.remarks: This setting only affects push-mode consumers (those with at least one sink registered via AddSink). Pull-mode consumers are unaffected.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.BackpressurePolicy.Block
member.summary: The router awaits until space becomes available in the lane. The reader loop blocks until the slow sink catches up. This is the default and recommended policy; no records are ever silently discarded.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.BackpressurePolicy.Drop
member.summary: The router calls and discards the record immediately if the lane is full. The reader loop never blocks; throughput is preserved at the cost of possible record loss. Dropped records are counted in and the pecl.sink.dropped_total metric (tagged by sink name). Use only when occasional record loss is acceptable.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.BackpressurePolicy.Spill
member.summary: When a sink lane is full, overflow records are written to a local spill file ({root}/spill/{consumer}-{sink}.spill) and replayed into the lane when space becomes available. No records are lost; adds disk I/O overhead.

remarks.para: Spill files use PECL record framing (RecordWriter.Write) and are crash-safe: if the pipeline restarts before replay completes, the spill file is re-replayed on next Start() (at-least-once delivery to ).
FIFO order is preserved: once a spill file exists for a lane, all subsequent records go to the spill file rather than the channel to prevent reordering.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.DurabilityMode
member.summary: Controls when the active segment file is flushed to the physical storage medium (fsync / FlushFileBuffers) during normal pipeline operation.
member.remarks: Regardless of this setting, always calls FlushToDisk, as do segment rollover and / . This enum only governs automatic flushing between those explicit events.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.DurabilityMode.None
member.summary: No automatic fsync between explicit calls, segment rollovers, and pipeline stop. Record bytes land in the OS page cache and are written to disk at the OS's discretion. Data may be lost on a hard process or OS crash before the OS flushes its cache. Suitable for development, testing, and workloads that can tolerate data loss.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.DurabilityMode.Batched
member.summary: A background timer calls FlushInternal directly at the interval configured by (default 100 ms), flushing the active segment to disk at a bounded frequency. Balances write throughput with bounded durability exposure. This is the default and recommended mode for most production workloads.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.DurabilityMode.Strict
member.summary: FlushToDisk is called after every . Provides the strongest durability guarantee — no appended record is lost on crash — at the cost of one fsync per record. Use for financial, audit, or compliance workloads where data loss is unacceptable.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.MissingCursorPolicy
member.summary: Controls the starting position for a consumer that has no cursor file when is called (first start, cursor deleted, or new consumer registered before a previous run).
member.remarks: The policy is applied only when the cursor file is absent or unreadable. Consumers with a valid existing cursor file always resume from their recorded position, regardless of this setting.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.MissingCursorPolicy.FromBeginning
member.summary: Position the consumer at the oldest available record in the log (beginning of the earliest retained segment). The consumer replays all existing records before processing new ones. This is the default.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.MissingCursorPolicy.FromTail
member.summary: Position the consumer at the current log tail. All records written before returns are skipped; only records appended after Start returns will be delivered. Use this for consumers that only care about new events (e.g. live alerting sinks that do not need historical replay).

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PeclDrainException
member.summary: Thrown (as an inner exception of AggregateException) by when a consumer's drain phase encounters a non- read error.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PeclDrainException.ConsumerName
member.summary: Name of the consumer that experienced the drain error.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PeclDrainException.DrainError
member.summary: The error returned by the segment reader during drain.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PeclDrainTimeoutException
member.summary: Thrown (as an inner exception of ) by when elapses before all push-mode consumer reader loops have drained to tail.
member.remarks: Cleanup (GC task cancel, writer dispose, consumer dispose) proceeds after the timeout fires, so the pipeline always reaches or state. Consumer cursors are persisted at their last flush position, not at the drain-end position.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PeclDrainTimeoutException.TimeoutMs
member.summary: The drain timeout that was exceeded, in milliseconds.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PeclDrainTimeoutException.TimedOutConsumers
member.summary: Names of consumers whose reader loops had not completed when the timeout fired.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PeclSeekException
member.summary: Thrown by when the target sequence number falls within a segment that has been deleted by retention GC.
member.remarks: The caller must inspect and either adjust the seek target or call to move to the earliest available position.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PeclSeekException.EarliestAvailableSeqNo
member.summary: The earliest sequence number currently available in the log.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline
member.summary: The top-level coordinator that wires together segment writing, checkpoint persistence, and consumer cursor tracking into a single crash-safe append-only log.

remarks.para: Lifecycle: transitions the pipeline from to after completing crash recovery. flushes and seals the active segment then transitions to again. calls automatically if the pipeline is still running.
Consumers must be registered via before calling for their persisted cursor positions to be recovered. Consumers registered after begin reading from position (segment 0, offset 0).
Thread-safety: not thread-safe. All calls must be made from a single thread or externally synchronized.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline._registeredConsumerNames
member.summary: Preserves the names of all consumers ever registered via . Unlike , this set is never cleared — it survives so that and can validate consumer names without requiring re-registration after each stop.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline._observabilityLock
member.summary: Guards observable gauge callbacks and .Clear() in to prevent the callback from iterating a collection being cleared concurrently.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline._checkpointLock
member.summary: Serialises all calls to so that the background Batched-mode flush timer and an explicit (or a rollover ) cannot open checkpoint.tmp concurrently. On Linux FileShare.None is enforced within the same process by the BCL's internal file-handle table, causing an on the second opener without this lock.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.State
member.summary: Current lifecycle state of the pipeline.

member.summary: Live operational counters for this pipeline instance.

member.summary: Appends a record to the log and returns its globally monotonic sequence number. If the active segment is full a new segment is opened first.
member.param: Raw payload bytes. May be empty.
Advisory payload encoding hint. Defaults to .
Optional schema identifier. Defaults to 0 (none).

length plus framing overhead exceeds . A single record must fit within one segment.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.AppendBlock(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte}},ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.ContentType,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Appends multiple payloads as a single block record and returns the block's globally monotonic sequence number. The block is stored as one framed record on disk with set; push-mode consumers receive the entries as individual instances (transparent to ). Pull-mode consumers receive the raw block record.
member.param: Payloads to encode into the block. Must contain at least one entry. All entries share the block frame's and .
Advisory payload encoding hint. Defaults to .
Optional schema identifier. Defaults to 0 (none).
member.returns: The sequence number assigned to the block record.
member.exception: is empty, or the encoded block payload plus framing overhead exceeds .
The pipeline is not in the state.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.Flush
member.summary: Flushes the active segment to disk, writes a checkpoint, and flushes all registered consumer cursors.
member.exception: The pipeline is not in the state.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.Stop
member.summary: Stops the pipeline: flushes, seals the active segment, disposes all consumers, and transitions to .
member.exception: One or more errors occurred during flush, drain, or GC-task shutdown. All resources have been released before this exception is thrown. The pipeline transitions to — it cannot be restarted. Construct a new instance to recover.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.ForceStop
member.summary: Immediately tears down the pipeline without waiting for consumer reader loops or sinks to drain. Reader cancellation tokens are cancelled and cleanup (GC task, writer, consumers) proceeds without any drain wait.
member.remarks:
The primary use case is escaping a call that has already timed out (pipeline is in state) or bypassing drain entirely when fast shutdown is required.
Callable from or . A no-op if already or .
Does not throw for drain errors (there is no drain). May throw for I/O errors during cleanup, wrapped in .
Consumer cursors are persisted at their last flush position — not at the end of the log, because no drain pass was performed.
Thread safety: safe to call from a different thread while is blocking in its drain wait (the intended escape-hatch scenario). All _consumers accesses in both methods are serialized under _observabilityLock: snapshots and clears the map atomically; the second caller iterates an empty snapshot.
If is already when is called, re-assigning Draining is intentional and a no-op — cleanup proceeds regardless of which call originally set the state.
member.exception: One or more I/O errors occurred during cleanup.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.ExecuteCleanupSequence(System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Exception})
member.summary: Runs the unconditional pipeline cleanup sequence: cancels and awaits the GC task, seals and disposes the writer, disposes all consumers, clears the consumer map, and sets to or .
member.remarks: Extracted from 's finally block so that can share the same cleanup path. _consumers is snapshotted and cleared atomically inside _observabilityLock so that a concurrent + invocation (the escape-hatch scenario) does not produce a from concurrent Dictionary modification. The second caller that reaches this method iterates an empty snapshot.
member.param: Accumulator for any errors encountered during cleanup.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.RegisterConsumer(System.String)
member.summary: Registers a consumer so it can read records via . Consumers registered before have their cursor positions recovered from disk. Consumers registered after begin at position (0, 0).
member.param: The unique name of the consumer. Must not be or empty.
member.exception: A consumer with the same name is already registered.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.AddSink(System.String,System.String,ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Sinks.ISink)
member.summary: Registers a sink for the named consumer. The pipeline will automatically deliver records to this sink when is called.

remarks.para: Must be called before ; the consumer must already be registered via . A consumer with at least one sink is in push mode: records are delivered automatically and is unavailable for that consumer.

member.param: The name of the pre-registered consumer.
A unique name for this sink within the consumer. Used for lane identification and diagnostics.
The sink implementation. Must not be .
member.exception: The pipeline is not in the state, or no consumer with is registered, or a sink named is already registered for that consumer.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.AddSink(System.String,System.String,ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Sinks.ISink,ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.ContentType)
member.summary: Registers a sink for the named consumer, routing only records whose matches . Records that do not match any registered content-type filter are dropped and the pipeline's drop counter is incremented with sink name "*".
member.remarks: All AddSink calls for a given consumer must use the same overload (broadcast, content-type, or hash). Mixing raises .
member.param: The name passed to .
A name unique within this consumer.
The sink implementation to receive filtered records.
The value a record must carry to be forwarded to this sink.
member.exception: The pipeline is not in the state, no consumer with is registered, a sink named is already registered for that consumer, or the consumer already uses a different routing strategy.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.AddSink(System.String,System.String,ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Sinks.ISink,System.Func{ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.LogRecord,System.Int32})
member.summary: Registers a sink for the named consumer using hash-based routing. Records are distributed across sinks by abs(keySelector(record)) % sinkCount.

remarks.para: All AddSink calls for a given consumer must use the same overload. Mixing raises .

member.param: The name passed to .
A name unique within this consumer.
The sink implementation to receive routed records.
A function that extracts an integer routing key from a record. The key is reduced modulo the number of registered sinks to select the target lane.
member.exception: The pipeline is not in the state, no consumer with is registered, a sink named is already registered for that consumer, or the consumer already uses a different routing strategy.

member.summary: Moves the named consumer's cursor to the first record with >= . The cursor is persisted to disk immediately.
member.remarks: The pipeline must be in the state. Call or before seeking. On the next , the consumer will resume from the seeked position.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.ResetConsumer(System.String)
member.summary: Resets the named consumer's cursor to the earliest available record in the log.

remarks.para: The pipeline must be in the state. When the log is empty, a zeroed cursor is written and the consumer will start from the beginning on the next .
When old segments have been deleted by retention GC, this method moves the cursor to the first record of the oldest remaining segment (not necessarily SeqNo 0).

member.param: The name passed to .
member.exception: The pipeline is not in the state, or is not registered.

member.summary: Blocks until the named consumer's drain error has been recorded by the reader loop, or until elapses. For use in tests only.

member.summary: Disposes the pipeline. If the pipeline is running it is stopped first. This method is idempotent.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.DeleteOrphanedSpillFiles
member.summary: Deletes spill files in {root}/spill/ that have no matching registered consumer+sink. Called at the end of setup so orphaned files from deregistered sinks do not accumulate on disk indefinitely.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.WriteCheckpointCore(System.UInt32,System.Int64,System.UInt64,System.UInt32)
member.remarks:
During segment rollover, this method is called after the new writer is opened. is the just-sealed segment; is the new segment ID. These values differ at rollover. On recovery, scanFromSegId = chk.LastSegmentId so the scan starts at the last sealed segment — correct because no records have been written to the new segment yet.
During a non-rollover flush, lastSegId == activeSegId == _writer.SegmentId.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.ConsumerState.CurrentSegmentId
member.summary: Segment the consumer will read from next.
member.remarks: Volatile: written by the main thread (pull-mode, ) or by the reader loop task (push-mode); read by the GC background task (). On 64-bit runtimes volatile uint reads and writes are atomic.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.ConsumerState.LastFullyRoutedSegmentId
member.summary: The segment ID of the segment from which the most recently routed record was read. Updated after RouteAsync completes for each record. Used as the GC watermark: the GC task may only delete segments strictly below Min(LastFullyRoutedSegmentId) across all consumers.
member.remarks: Volatile: written exclusively by the reader loop task; read by the GC background task. On 64-bit runtimes volatile uint reads and writes are atomic. Initial value 0 is safe — segment 0 is not deleted until at least one record has been routed from it.

member.summary: Pairs a named with its dedicated and running drain . Also owns the single-element rented from that is reused across every call to avoid per-record allocation.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.SinkSlot.SinkName
member.summary: Unique name of this sink within the consumer.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.SinkSlot.Sink
member.summary: The sink implementation that receives batches.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.SinkSlot.Lane
member.summary: Bounded channel decoupling the reader loop from sink I/O.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.SinkSlot.Task
member.summary: The running sink drain task; until .

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.SinkSlot.WriteBuffer
member.summary: Single-element buffer rented from once at construction. Element [0] is overwritten before each call. Returned to the pool in after all sink tasks have completed. Use (not this array) when calling WriteAsync to avoid exposing excess rented capacity.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.Pipeline.SinkSlot.WriteSegment
member.summary: An view over element [0] of , stored as so the boxing occurs once at construction rather than on every call.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration
member.summary: Immutable configuration for a instance.
member.remarks: All paths are resolved relative to . The pipeline creates segments/ and cursors/ subdirectories automatically on first .

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.RootDirectory
member.summary: Absolute path to the root directory that contains the pipeline's segments/, cursors/, and checkpoint.dat.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.MaxSegmentSize
member.summary: Maximum number of bytes a single segment file may grow to before a new segment is opened. Defaults to 64 MiB.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.CursorFlushRecordThreshold
member.summary: Number of records a consumer must advance before its cursor is automatically flushed to disk. Defaults to 1 000.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.CursorFlushInterval
member.summary: Maximum time that may elapse between automatic cursor flushes when the record threshold has not been reached. Defaults to 5 seconds.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.DurabilityMode
member.summary: Controls when the active segment is flushed to the physical storage medium during normal pipeline operation. Defaults to .
member.remarks: , segment rollover, and / always call FlushToDisk regardless of this setting. This property only governs automatic flushing between those explicit events.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.FsyncIntervalMs
member.summary: Interval in milliseconds between automatic segment flushes when is . Ignored for and . Defaults to 100 ms. Use a small value (e.g. 50) in tests to trigger the flush timer quickly without relying on an explicit call.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.SinkLaneCapacity
member.summary: Maximum number of instances that may be buffered in each before the reader loop blocks (Block backpressure). Defaults to 4,096.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.BackpressurePolicy
member.summary: Backpressure policy applied when a push-mode sink lane is at capacity. Defaults to .
member.remarks: Under , the reader loop awaits space in the lane, guaranteeing no record loss at the cost of blocking throughput. Under , the record is discarded immediately and is incremented. Pull-mode consumers are unaffected by this setting.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.DrainTimeoutMs
member.summary: Maximum time in milliseconds that will wait for all push-mode consumer reader loops to drain before proceeding with cleanup. 0 means unbounded (the pre-MILESTONE-07 behaviour). Defaults to 30,000 ms.
member.remarks: When the timeout fires, a is added to the thrown by and cleanup proceeds immediately. Consumer cursors are persisted at their last flush position. Set to a small value (e.g., 200) in tests to avoid long waits on deliberately blocked sinks.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.RetentionPolicy
member.summary: Retention policy that controls when sealed segments become eligible for GC. Defaults to .

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.RetentionMaxAgeMs
member.summary: Maximum age of a sealed segment in milliseconds before it becomes eligible for deletion under . Defaults to 604,800,000 ms (7 days). Ignored for other retention policies.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.RetentionMaxBytes
member.summary: Maximum total on-disk size in bytes of all segments before the oldest sealed segments are deleted under . Defaults to 1,073,741,824 bytes (1 GiB). Ignored for other retention policies.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.GcIntervalMs
member.summary: Approximate interval in milliseconds between GC passes. The GC background task sleeps for this duration between sweeps. Defaults to 60,000 (60 s). Use a small value (e.g. 50) in tests to avoid long sleeps.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.GcStopTimeoutMs
member.summary: Maximum time in milliseconds that and will wait for the GC background task to complete after cancellation is requested. Defaults to 5 000 ms.
member.remarks: If the GC task does not complete within this window (e.g., because File.Delete is blocking on a hung network mount), cleanup proceeds without it. The task will terminate on its own once the filesystem recovers. Set to a small value (e.g., 200) in tests to avoid long waits.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.MeterName
member.summary: Name of the registered by this pipeline. Use a unique value when running multiple pipelines in the same process to avoid instrument name collisions. Defaults to "pecl".

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.CompressionAlgorithm
member.summary: Compression algorithm applied to record payloads before writing to disk. Defaults to (no compression).
member.remarks: Compression is transparent to consumers — always contains decompressed bytes. When is configured, payloads that do not compress (compressed size >= original) are stored uncompressed automatically.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineConfiguration.MissingCursorPolicy
member.summary: Determines the starting position for a consumer whose cursor file is absent on (first start, cursor deleted, or new consumer). Defaults to .
member.remarks:
replays all records from the oldest available segment — the safe default for new consumers.
skips all existing records; only records appended after returns are delivered. Use this for consumers that only care about new events and do not need historical replay.
This property is ignored when a valid cursor file already exists for the consumer.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics
member.summary: Exposes live operational counters for a instance and publishes them as instruments on a named .

remarks.para: All counter values are also updated atomically via and are safe to read from any thread.
Call (or let do it automatically) to release the underlying .

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initialises a new and registers a with the given name.
member.param: Name passed to . Use a unique value per pipeline instance when running multiple pipelines in the same process.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.Meter
member.summary: The registered by this instance. Exposed for test assertion via .

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.RecordsAppended
member.summary: Total number of records appended to the log since the pipeline was last started. Incremented by .

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.BytesAppended
member.summary: Total payload bytes appended to the log since the pipeline was last started. Incremented by using the raw payload length (excluding framing overhead).

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.FlushCount
member.summary: Number of times the active segment was explicitly flushed to the OS page cache. Incremented by every internal flush (explicit call, segment rollover, or ).

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.SegmentRollovers
member.summary: Number of segment rollovers that have occurred since the pipeline was last started. Incremented when seals the active segment and opens a new one because IsFull was .

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.RecoveryCount
member.summary: Number of times crash recovery ran. Incremented once per successful call.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.RecoveryTruncatedBytes
member.summary: Total bytes removed from the tail segment during the most recent recovery scan. Zero when the log was clean (no truncation required). Reset to zero on each start.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.SegmentsDeleted
member.summary: Total number of segment files deleted by the GC background task since the pipeline was last started.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.SegmentCount
member.summary: Number of segment files present in the segments directory as of the last GC pass (or pipeline start if GC has not yet run).

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.SinkDropped
member.summary: Total records discarded because a sink lane was full and was active. Zero when is used.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.ConsumerReadRate
member.summary: Total records successfully read via since the pipeline was last started.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.RecoveryDurationMs
member.summary: Duration of the most recent recovery scan in milliseconds. Reset to zero at each .

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.SegmentBytes
member.summary: Total on-disk bytes across all segment files as of the last GC pass (or pipeline start if GC has not yet run).

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineMetrics.StartObservableInstruments(System.Func{System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Diagnostics.Metrics.Measurement{System.Int64}}},System.Func{System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Diagnostics.Metrics.Measurement{System.Int64}}})
member.summary: Registers instruments for consumer lag, sink lane depth, and segment count. Idempotent — only the first call registers instruments; subsequent calls are no-ops.
member.param: Callback that returns one per push-mode consumer, tagged with "consumer".
Callback that returns one per sink lane, tagged with "consumer" and "sink".
member.remarks: The pecl.consumer.lag gauge reports measurements for push-mode consumers only (consumers registered with at least one sink via AddSink). Pull-mode consumers do not set LastRoutedSeqNo and are excluded from this gauge. A pull-mode consumer that is far behind the log tail will not appear in this metric.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineState
member.summary: Lifecycle state of a instance.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineState.Starting
member.summary: The pipeline is performing recovery and preparing internal state. No I/O operations are accepted in this state.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineState.Running
member.summary: The pipeline is fully operational. Append, Flush, and ReadNext are all valid in this state.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineState.Draining
member.summary: Stop has been called. The pipeline is flushing and sealing the active segment. No new appends are accepted.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineState.Stopped
member.summary: The pipeline has been cleanly shut down. All data is durable on disk.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.PipelineState.Error
member.summary: An unrecoverable error occurred. The pipeline cannot be used further.
member.remarks: This is a terminal state. Once a pipeline enters Error, will throw . To recover, dispose the current instance and construct a new with the same configuration; the new instance will run crash recovery on .

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.RetentionPolicy
member.summary: Controls when sealed segments become eligible for garbage collection.
member.remarks: is the default and safest policy. and delete segments regardless of consumer read positions and should only be used when occasional data loss for lagging consumers is acceptable.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.RetentionPolicy.ConsumerGated
member.summary: A segment is eligible for deletion only when every registered consumer has advanced its read position strictly past the segment's end. This is the default and safest policy: no data reachable by any consumer is ever deleted.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.RetentionPolicy.TimeBased
member.summary: Sealed segments whose last-write timestamp is older than are eligible for deletion, regardless of consumer read positions. Suitable for telemetry or log pipelines where a bounded retention window is required and slow consumers may lose data.
member.remarks: Segment age is measured by File.GetLastWriteTimeUtc, which reflects when the last byte was written to the segment file (effectively its seal time). The active (writable) segment is never deleted.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Pipeline.RetentionPolicy.SizeBased
member.summary: Oldest sealed segments are deleted when the total on-disk size of all segments (including the active segment) exceeds . Suitable for pipelines with strict disk-space budgets. Lagging consumers may lose data.
member.remarks: Segments are deleted in ascending order (oldest first) until the total size falls within the configured limit. The active (writable) segment is never deleted.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.BlockPayloadReader
member.summary: Decodes a block payload produced by into individual entry payloads. Designed as a for zero-allocation, stack-only sequential decoding.

remarks.para: Construct with the raw block payload span (the bytes stored after the record header). Call in a loop until it returns .
The returned slices are valid only as long as the original buffer passed to the constructor is alive.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.BlockPayloadReader.#ctor(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Initialises a new over .
member.param: The raw block payload bytes — the 4-byte entry count followed by the encoded entries.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.BlockPayloadReader.EntryCount
member.summary: Total number of entries encoded in the block payload.

member.summary: Reads the next entry payload from the block.
member.param: On success, a over the entry bytes. The span is a slice of the original buffer passed to the constructor.
member.returns: when an entry was successfully read; when all entries have been consumed or the buffer is malformed.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.BlockPayloadWriter
member.summary: Encodes a list of raw byte payloads into a single block payload suitable for storage as a record.
member.remarks:
Block payload wire format (all multi-byte fields little-endian):
[4 bytes: entry count (uint)] for each entry: [4 bytes: payload length (int)] [payload bytes]
All entries share the block frame's ContentType and SchemaId.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.BlockPayloadWriter.HeaderSize
member.summary: Byte size of the block header (entry count field).

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.BlockPayloadWriter.EntryLengthSize
member.summary: Byte size of the per-entry length prefix.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.BlockPayloadWriter.ComputeSize(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte}})
member.summary: Computes the total encoded byte length for a block payload containing .

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.BlockPayloadWriter.Write(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte}},System.Span{System.Byte})
member.summary: Writes the block payload encoding of into . The span must be exactly bytes long.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.CompressionAlgorithm
member.summary: Compression algorithm applied to record payloads before writing to disk.
member.remarks: Compression is transparent to consumers — always contains decompressed bytes. Configure via .

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.CompressionAlgorithm.None
member.summary: No compression. Default.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.CompressionAlgorithm.Brotli
member.summary: Brotli compression at quality level 1. Uses . Payloads that do not compress (compressed size >= original) are stored uncompressed automatically.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.ContentType
member.summary: Advisory hint stored at byte offset 6 of a PECL record header describing how the payload bytes are encoded.
member.remarks: PECL does not interpret the payload. This field is purely advisory: consumers use it to select the appropriate deserializer. Writers that do not use schema integration should pass or the most appropriate value.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.ContentType.Unknown
member.summary: Encoding is unspecified or unknown. Consumers must determine encoding out-of-band.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.ContentType.Json
member.summary: UTF-8 encoded JSON.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.ContentType.MessagePack
member.summary: MessagePack binary encoding.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.ContentType.Protobuf
member.summary: Protocol Buffers (protobuf) binary encoding.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.ContentType.Custom
member.summary: Application-defined encoding. The meaning is entirely up to the producer and consumer.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.CrcUtility
member.summary: Internal helper for computing CRC-32 over the two-span PECL record data (serialized header bytes followed by payload bytes).
member.remarks: Uses CRC-32/ISO-HDLC () from System.IO.Hashing. See ADR-001 for the rationale behind choosing this algorithm over CRC-32C.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.CrcUtility.ComputeRecordCrc32C(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Computes the CRC-32/ISO-HDLC integrity check for a record's header and payload.
member.param: The 24 serialized header bytes.
The record payload bytes. May be empty.
member.remarks: Despite the name suffix "Crc32C", this method uses CRC-32/ISO-HDLC (), not CRC-32C (Castagnoli). The method name is retained per ADR-001 to avoid a cascade of renames. Any external tool validating PECL record integrity must use CRC-32/ISO-HDLC.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.CrcUtility.ComputeCrc32(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Computes CRC-32 over a single contiguous span of bytes.
member.param: The bytes to checksum.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.RecordFlags
member.summary: Bit flags stored at byte offset 5 of a PECL record header. Controls record-type classification and compression.
member.remarks:
Bit layout:
Bit 0 — : multi-entry block record (reserved for future use)
Bit 1 — : payload is compressed
Bits 2–3 — /: algorithm id (00=None, 01=Brotli)
Bits 4–7 — reserved, must be zero
Readers should ignore unknown bits to allow forward compatibility.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.RecordFlags.None
member.summary: No flags set; single uncompressed record.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.RecordFlags.IsBlock
member.summary: The payload is a block record: it contains multiple logical entries preceded by a block-level header. Defined for future use; Phase 1 writers always produce .

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.RecordFlags.IsCompressed
member.summary: The payload is compressed. The compression algorithm is encoded in bits 2–3 ( and ). Use to decode.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.RecordFlags.CompressionAlg0
member.summary: Compression algorithm bit 0 (LSB of the 2-bit algorithm field in bits 2–3).

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.RecordFlags.CompressionAlg1
member.summary: Compression algorithm bit 1 (MSB of the 2-bit algorithm field in bits 2–3).

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.RecordFlagsExtensions
member.summary: Extension helpers for .

member.summary: Extracts the encoded in bits 2–3 of . Returns when is clear.

OffsetSizeField
04 B

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.RecordWriter
member.summary: Serializes a single PECL record to a stream.
member.remarks: A record consists of a 24-byte fixed header (), a variable-length payload, and a 4-byte CRC32C footer (). The CRC32C is computed over both the serialized header bytes and the payload bytes.

member.summary: Total fixed framing overhead per record in bytes (header + footer), excluding the payload.

Monotonically increasing sequence number. The caller (pipeline writer task) is responsible for assigning correct values; this method stores whatever is passed.
Advisory payload encoding hint. Default: .
Record-level flags. Default: .
Schema registry identifier. Pass 0 when unused.
Compression algorithm to apply to the payload before writing. When the compressed size is not smaller than the original the record is stored uncompressed. Default: .
member.returns: Total bytes written to : + effective payload length (which may differ from payload.Length when compression reduces the size). The return type is to avoid overflow for near-maximum payloads.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentNaming
member.summary: Encapsulates the segment file naming convention: log-NNNNNN.seg where NNNNNN is the zero-padded six-digit segment identifier.

remarks.para: Six-digit zero-padding ensures lexicographic sort order matches numeric order for segment IDs 0 through 999,999 — sufficient for all practical log lifetimes.
Invariants:

list.item: Filenames are always lower-case ASCII.
The format is stable; any change requires a new ADR entry.

member.name: F:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentNaming.MaxSegmentId
member.summary: The maximum valid segment identifier (999,999). PECL filenames use a 6-digit zero-padded format (log-NNNNNN.seg), so IDs ≥ 1,000,000 would produce unparseable 7-digit filenames and must be rejected at write time.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentNaming.GetFileName(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Returns the filename (no directory) for the given segment identifier.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentNaming.GetFilePath(System.String,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Returns the full file path for the given segment in .

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentNaming.TryParseId(System.String,System.UInt32@)
member.summary: Attempts to parse the segment identifier from a segment filename.
member.param: The bare filename (no directory), e.g. "log-000042.seg".
Set to the parsed identifier on success.
member.returns: if the filename matches the expected pattern.

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentReader
member.summary: Performs a sequential forward scan of a segment file, deserialising and CRC-validating each framed record via .

remarks.para: A opens the segment file for reading and advances its position with each successful call. The file is opened with so that a concurrent may continue appending to the same segment.
End-of-segment is signalled by a with code and zero bytes read — callers should treat this as a clean stop rather than an error when scanning a complete or in-progress segment.
Invariants:

list.item: is monotonically non-decreasing.
The reader never writes to the segment file.
After disposal, throws .

remarks.para: Failure modes: file-not-found propagates from the constructor. I/O errors during reading propagate from . Corruption (bad CRC, truncated header/payload) is returned as a failure rather than thrown.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentReader.SegmentId
member.summary: The identifier of the segment this reader scans.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentReader.Position
member.summary: Current byte position within the segment file.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentReader.#ctor(System.String,System.UInt32,System.Int64)
member.summary: Opens the segment file for sequential reading, optionally seeking to before the first call.
member.param: Absolute path to the directory that holds segment files.
Numeric identifier of the segment to read.
Byte offset within the segment at which to begin reading. Defaults to 0 (start of file).
member.exception: The segment file does not exist.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentReader.ReadNext
member.summary: Reads and validates the next framed record from the segment.
member.returns: with the deserialized record on success; with a describing the failure otherwise. A failure with zero bytes read indicates end-of-segment (clean stop).

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentReader.Dispose
member.summary: Closes the underlying .

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter
member.summary: Appends framed records to a single segment file, tracking write position and signalling when the segment has reached its configured size limit.

remarks.para: A owns exactly one for the segment file identified by . It does not manage segment rollover itself — callers (the Pipeline) check after each and create a new writer for the next segment.
Invariants:

list.item: is monotonically non-decreasing.
equals the on-disk file length when first opened.
is stable once .
After , always throws.
After disposal, all mutating methods throw .

remarks.para: Failure modes: directory-not-found and I/O errors from propagate to the caller unchanged. is not updated if throws.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter.SegmentId
member.summary: The identifier of the segment this writer manages.

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter.BytesWritten
member.summary: Total bytes written to this segment since it was created or last opened.
member.remarks: Written exclusively by the main thread (via ); read by the reader loop background task (via ). All reads use Volatile.Read and all writes use Volatile.Write to provide the required memory-ordering guarantees. The C# volatile keyword cannot be applied to long fields, so the Volatile.* helpers are used instead. Safe on 64-bit runtimes; not guaranteed atomic on 32-bit (PECL targets .NET 9 / x64).

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter.Position
member.summary: Current write position within the segment file. Equals .

member.name: P:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter.IsFull
member.summary: when has reached or exceeded the configured maximum segment size. No further calls are permitted.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter.#ctor(System.String,System.UInt32,System.Int64,ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Records.CompressionAlgorithm)
member.summary: Opens or creates the segment file for the given identifier and prepares it for appending. If the file already exists, writing resumes at the current end.
member.param: Absolute path to the directory that holds segment files. The directory must already exist; it is not created automatically.
Numeric identifier of this segment.
Maximum number of bytes this segment may hold. Must be at least (28 bytes — enough for one empty record).
Compression algorithm applied to every record appended to this segment. Defaults to .
member.exception: exceeds . PECL segment filenames use a 6-digit zero-padded ID; IDs ≥ 1,000,000 cannot be represented.

member.summary: Serialises as a framed record and appends it to the segment.
member.param: Raw payload bytes. May be empty.
Globally monotonic sequence number assigned by the writer task.
Advisory payload encoding hint. Defaults to .
Record flags. Defaults to .

member.exception: The segment is sealed or is .

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter.Flush
member.summary: Flushes any buffered data to the operating system page cache. Does not guarantee durability — use an fsync at the Pipeline layer for that.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter.FlushToDisk
member.summary: Flushes all buffered segment data to the physical storage medium (fsync / FlushFileBuffers). Call this before writing a checkpoint to guarantee the checkpoint invariant: a checkpoint always points to data that is durable on disk.
member.remarks: Unlike , which only flushes to the OS page cache, this method blocks until the OS confirms the data is on the storage device.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter.Seal
member.summary: Seals the segment: flushes buffered data and prevents further calls. After sealing, the file remains open until is called. Calling on an already-sealed or disposed writer is a no-op.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Segments.SegmentWriter.Dispose
member.summary: Seals (if not already sealed) and closes the underlying .

member.name: T:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Sinks.ISink
member.summary: Delivers batches of records to an external system.

remarks.para: In the current MVP each batch contains exactly one record; future phases may group multiple records into a single batch to amortise per-call overhead.

Implementations must not return from until the batch has been durably committed to the external system. Any exception thrown propagates to the pipeline's error handling layer and may cause the affected consumer to enter an error state.

member.name: M:ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Sinks.ISink.WriteAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{ByTech.EmbeddedCommitLog.Consumer.LogRecord},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Writes to the external system. Must not return until the batch is durably committed (or throw on failure).
member.param: Records to write. Never null or empty during normal operation.
Cancellation token; cancellation should abort the in-flight write and propagate.
